pi_items.source: File inspection of 4StoreyBuilding.inp in task data directory
File inspection confirms Ground Floor spaces use 'G' prefix (e.g., 'South Perim Spc (G.S11)', 'Ground Flr')
File inspection confirms South-facing surfaces exist with 'S' suffix in space names (e.g., 'South Perim Spc', 'South Wall (G.S11.E1)')
Task specification for new door; existing door in file has 5.5 ft width at location (G.S11.E1.D1)

Task specification for new door; existing door in file has 6.5 ft height at location (G.S11.E1.D1); standard door height per building code is 6'8" (80 inches), so 7 feet is non-standard but valid for custom doors
Web search of ASHRAE 90.1 standards confirms 0.40 Btu/(h·ft²·°F) is reasonable for insulated steel/metal door construction, exceeding standard requirements of 0.60 for typical opaque doors
Task-specified tolerance range (0.38-0.42 Btu/(h·ft²·°F)); reasonable engineering tolerance for construction specification

Path follows eQUEST 3.65 standard Windows installation convention per official documentation; verified through setup scripts which reference 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\eQUEST 3-65 Projects' as the standard projects directory

privileged_info_summary: The 4StoreyBuilding synthetic eQUEST model uses a naming convention where Ground Floor spaces use 'G' prefix and South-facing surfaces use 'S' suffix. The existing building model already contains a door on the south facade at location (G.S11.E1.D1) with dimensions of 5.5 ft width × 6.5 ft height. The task specifies creation of a new door with dimensions 6.0 ft width × 7.0 ft height (non-standard per typical building codes but valid for custom commercial doors) with U-value of 0.40 ± 0.02 Btu/(h·ft²·°F), which represents good thermal performance exceeding ASHRAE 90.1 standard requirements for insulated metal doors. The project path follows eQUEST 3.65 standard Windows directory structure.
